title: corydalis afghanica subsp. tenuis
intro: Corydalis afghanica subsp. tenuis, also known as Afghan corydalis, is a type of flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Afghanistan and the Himalayas. It belongs to the poppy family and is recognized for its medicinal properties, including its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, such as pain, anxiety, and insomnia, due to its ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. Corydalis afghanica subsp. tenuis can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
